hero.question: Will Google have the best AI model at the end of March 2026?
hero.prob_take:
The 4.5 point crater in 24 hours signals serious conviction shorting, likely from traders who've seen Gemini 2.5's underwhelming performance versus Claude and GPT-4o in head-to-head evals. Google's still competitive but they're chasing, not leading, and March 2026 is only 15 months of runway for a company that's been iterating quietly while Anthropic and OpenAI dominate the narrative.

6.5% is gift pricing if you believe Google drops a legitimately best-in-class model before EOQ1 2026, which their release cadence and capital suggest is more likely than the market's pricing,

daily_take.headline: Google's AI Crown Slips to 6.5% - Market Finally Prices in Reality
daily_take.deck: Google dropped 4.5 points in 24 hours as the market wakes up to a brutal truth: Anthropic's Claude and OpenAI's o1 are running circles around Gemini. The 6.5% price is actually reasonable given Google's track record of overpromising and the March 2026 deadline favoring proven players over vaporware. Smart money already exited, so don't chase shorts. The real alpha is staying flat and watching for a desperation announcement from Google that might create a 9-10% spike to fade.
